title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Why NRIs in Singapore Still Buy Indian Life Insurance in 2025?
With world-class architecture, diverse culture, and an efficient infrastructure, Singapore is among the advanced and financially stable countries in the world. This makes it an attractive destination for Indians, be it for work, education, or long-term settlement. Despite all this, for many NRIs living in Singapore, staying connected to their roots comes naturally. It becomes all the more important when you have loved ones in India.
To financially safeguard your family members back home, life insurance can be the easiest and simplest way forward. Thanks to digitisation in the insurance industry in India, buying the right coverage has become simpler. This, along with factors like affordable premiums, better coverage, etc., are reasons why so many NRIs purchase Indian life insurance policies.
The changing dynamics of the Indian life insurance industry have now made it easier for NRIs to purchase policies and benefit from it. There are different types of policies that meet the specific needs of different families, which may further attract many NRIs.
This guide discusses all the reasons why NRIs in Singapore choose Indian life insurance policies. Read on to make an informed decision.
Reasons Why NRIs in Singapore Buy Indian Life Insurance
Life insurance has become a basic necessity for most people. Amid the uncertainties of life and the increasing awareness of life insurance, many people are now acknowledging the importance of life insurance.
Indian life insurance for NRIs in Singapore can be a meaningful choice for many reasons. Read on as we discuss the reasons behind it:
Premium Cost vs Singapore Policies
One of the major concerns while buying a life insurance policy is the premium cost. Indian life insurance plans are comparatively more affordable for NRIs in Singapore. Wondering how? Let's understand.
The difference between the Singapore Dollar and the Indian Rupee is significant. Singapore Dollar is stronger than the Indian Rupee (INR). Since insurance plans in India are INR denominated, the premiums prove affordable for NRIs earning in Singapore Dollars.
Claim Support System in India
Under the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA), Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) are permitted to purchase life insurance policies in India. This provision enables NRIs to secure financial protection for their families, regardless of their country of residence.
The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) serves as the apex regulatory body for the insurance sector in India. IRDAI ensures that insurance companies operate transparently, ethically, and in the best interests of policyholders. It monitors solvency margins, enforces fair claim settlement practices, and mandates public disclosure of claim settlement ratios to promote accountability
As a result, the claim support system in India is robust and responsive. Insurance companies are required to resolve customer queries and grievances promptly, thereby minimizing inconvenience and enhancing trust in the system
If you have family back home, your nominee can file a claim with the Indian insurer easily in the case of your demise. The insurance company would process the  claim and settle it at the earliest so that your family in India can get the death benefit when needed. Moreover, the death benefit is paid in INR which makes it easier for your family to use the benefits immediately without the added step of currency conversion.
Digital Onboarding from Singapore
Indian life insurance products are not only affordable in terms of premium, but also because of digital onboarding. As an NRI in Singapore, you do not necessarily have to visit India to purchase a life insurance policy. NRIs in Singapore can purchase Indian life insurance online from the comfort of their residence or office in Singapore.
To purchase the policy, you need to fill out and submit the proposal  form online on the official website of your chosen insurance provider. You will also have to attach documents as required by the insurer, including a medical check-up report. You may be required to undergo a medical check-up in Singapore itself and send the reports online to your insurer.
This digital comfort of purchasing life insurance breaks the barrier of geographical distances. Now, you can purchase Indian life insurance from anywhere in the world.
Multiple Types of Life Insurance Plans
NRIs in Singapore can choose from a range of life insurance plans. Some of the most popular options are:
Endowment Plans
Endowment plans offer both life insurance and savings. Along with the assurance of coverage, they provide a payout on maturity, making them ideal for long-term financial goals like education, marriage, or retirement.
Term Plans
These are pure life insurance plans  with no savings element. At affordable premiums, you can get access to a comprehensive  sum assured .
Money-back Plans
In this type of life insurance plan, you get regular payouts during the policy term along with life insurance coverage. This helps in creating liquidity.
Child Insurance Plan
Get life insurance and build a secure financial corpus for your child with a child insurance plan. The policy secures the financial future of the child even if the parent is no longer around.
Tax Benefits in India
Apart from the aforementioned reasons, you can also avail yourself of various tax benefits by purchasing Indian life insurance policies. Take a look:
NRIs in Singapore can waive off the GST subject to eligibility criteria and submission of documents every year that is charged on the life insurance products. If the premium payment for the life insurance is done through an NRE (Non-Resident External) account
Section 80C of the Income Tax Act of 1961 lets you avail tax deductions of up to ₹1.5 lakhs against the premiums that are paid
Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act of 1961 makes the maturity benefit tax-free, subject to specific conditions.
The death benefit is always tax-free.
FAQs
Can NRIs purchase Indian life insurance from Singapore?
Yes, you can digitally purchase Indian life insurance from Singapore. You may also purchase the policy when you visit India.
Is the life insurance death benefit taxable in India?
No, the life insurance death benefit is tax-free in India.
How can NRIs pay life insurance premiums?
NRIs in Singapore can pay life insurance premiums in India using their NRE (Non-Resident External), NRO (Non-Resident Ordinary) accounts, foreign bank accounts, or international cards ot swift transactions.
Which is the best life insurance plan for NRIs in Singapore?
There is no one-size-fits-all life insurance plan. There are different choices available, and you need to consider your family's specific financial needs to choose the most suitable one. Some plans offer pure life cover, while some have savings, market linked investments in addition to the life cover etc. So, choose smartly.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions, please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in
Tax benefits as per prevailing Section 10(10D) and Section 80C (under old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act shall apply. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy

The changing dynamics of the Indian life insurance industry have now made it easier for NRIs to purchase policies and benefit from it. There are different types of policies that meet the specific needs of different families, which may further attract many NRIs. This guide discusses all the reasons why NRIs in Singapore choose Indian life insurance policies. Read on to make an informed decision. Reasons Why NRIs in Singapore Buy Indian Life Insurance Life insurance has become a basic necessity for most people. Amid the uncertainties of life and the increasing awareness of life insurance, many people are now acknowledging the importance of life insurance. Indian life insurance for NRIs in Singapore can be a meaningful choice for many reasons. Read on as we discuss the reasons behind it:
● Premium Cost vs Singapore Policies
One of the major concerns while buying a life insurance policy is the premium cost. Indian life insurance plans are comparatively more affordable for NRIs in Singapore. Wondering how? Let's understand. The difference between the Singapore Dollar and the Indian Rupee is significant. Singapore Dollar is stronger than the Indian Rupee (INR). IRDAI ensures that insurance companies operate transparently, ethically, and in the best interests of policyholders. It monitors solvency margins, enforces fair claim settlement practices, and mandates public disclosure of claim settlement ratios to promote accountability As a result, the claim support system in India is robust and responsive. Insurance companies are required to resolve customer queries and grievances promptly, thereby minimizing inconvenience and enhancing trust in the system If you have family back home, your nominee can file a claim with the Indian insurer easily in the case of your demise. The insurance company would process the claim and settle it at the earliest so that your family in India can get the death benefit when needed. You may be required to undergo a medical check-up in Singapore itself and send the reports online to your insurer. This digital comfort of purchasing life insurance breaks the barrier of geographical distances. Now, you can purchase Indian life insurance from anywhere in the world.
● Multiple Types of Life Insurance Plans
NRIs in Singapore can choose from a range of life insurance plans. Some of the most popular options are:
● Endowment Plans
Endowment plans offer both life insurance and savings. Along with the assurance of coverage, they provide a payout on maturity, making them ideal for long-term financial goals like education, marriage, or retirement. ● Term Plans These are pure life insurance plans with no savings element. At affordable premiums, you can get access to a comprehensive sum assured . ● Money-back Plans In this type of life insurance plan, you get regular payouts during the policy term along with life insurance coverage. This helps in creating liquidity. ● Child Insurance Plan Get life insurance and build a secure financial corpus for your child with a child insurance plan. The policy secures the financial future of the child even if the parent is no longer around.
● Tax Benefits in India
Apart from the aforementioned reasons, you can also avail yourself of various tax benefits by purchasing Indian life insurance policies. Take a look:
● NRIs in Singapore can waive off the GST subject to eligibility criteria and submission of documents every year that is charged on the life insurance products. If the premium payment for the life insurance is done through an NRE (Non-Resident External) account ● Section 80C of the Income Tax Act of 1961 lets you avail tax deductions of up to ₹1.5 lakhs against the premiums that are paid ● Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act of 1961 makes the maturity benefit tax-free, subject to specific conditions. ● The death benefit is always tax-free.

1. Can NRIs purchase Indian life insurance from Singapore?
Yes, you can digitally purchase Indian life insurance from Singapore. You may also purchase the policy when you visit India.
2. Is the life insurance death benefit taxable in India?

3. How can NRIs pay life insurance premiums?
NRIs in Singapore can pay life insurance premiums in India using their NRE (Non-Resident External), NRO (Non-Resident Ordinary) accounts, foreign bank accounts, or international cards ot swift transactions.
4. Which is the best life insurance plan for NRIs in Singapore?
